pkg/ffx.purpose: Provides a minimal feature flag service with three implementations (static, context-based, test hybrid). The primary constraint is that all feature-flag reads go through the Service interface so callers are decoupled from the backing store.

patterns.name: Service interface for all reads
patterns.description: All consumers call Service.IsFeatureEnabled(ctx, Feature) — never inspect AccessConfig directly outside this package.
patterns.example:
svc := ffx.NewStaticService(ffx.AccessConfig{MyFeature: true})
enabled, err := svc.IsFeatureEnabled(ctx, MyFeature)

patterns.name: Context-carried access config
patterns.description: At request time, SetAccessOnContext injects an AccessConfig into ctx; NewContextService reads it back via GetAccessFromContext. Missing key returns ErrContextMissing, not false.

patterns.name: Test hybrid service falls back to static
patterns.description: NewTestContextService tries ctx first, then falls back to the provided static defaults — lets tests override individual flags without rebuilding context per case.

patterns.name: Feature typed as string alias
patterns.description: Feature is type Feature string — always declare named Feature constants, never pass raw strings to IsFeatureEnabled.

key_file_guides.role: Declares Feature, AccessConfig, and the Service interface. Single source of truth for the package contract.
key_file_guides.watch_for: AccessConfig.Merge mutates the receiver — always pass a fresh copy if merging should not modify the original.

key_file_guides.role: SetAccessOnContext/GetAccessFromContext + contextService + testContextService. ErrContextMissing is the sentinel for missing ctx key.
key_file_guides.watch_for: contextService returns an error if the feature key is absent from the config; callers must handle that error, not just check the bool.

key_file_guides.role: staticService backed by an in-process AccessConfig map. Used for server startup wiring and test defaults.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Returns error (not false) for unknown features — do not assume unknown == disabled.

anti_patterns: Bypassing Service by reading AccessConfig maps directly outside this package
Using raw string literals instead of typed Feature constants
Ignoring the error return from IsFeatureEnabled (unknown feature != false)
Storing AccessConfig in a global variable instead of in context or Wire-injected service

decisions.decision: Three separate service implementations (static, context, test hybrid) rather than a single configurable one.
decisions.rationale: Static is safe for server startup; context-based serves per-request tenant flags; the test hybrid lets unit tests override without rebuilding context, all without introducing nil checks or type switches in callers.

code_examples.scenario: Wire a static service at startup and use it in a handler

pkg/filter.purpose: Provides typed, composable query filters (string, integer, float, time, boolean, ULID) that compile to both Ent selector predicates and go-sqlbuilder WHERE expressions. The primary constraint is that every filter type implements the Filter interface and enforces single-operator-per-node validation.

patterns.name: Filter interface dual output
patterns.description: Every filter type implements both Select(field string) func(*sql.Selector) for Ent queries and SelectWhereExpr(field string, q *sqlbuilder.SelectBuilder) string for ClickHouse/raw SQL. New filter types must implement both.
patterns.example:
predicate := myFilter.Select("name")  // -> func(*sql.Selector)
expr := myFilter.SelectWhereExpr("name", q)  // -> string

patterns.name: Single-operator validation
patterns.description: validateSingleOperator(f) uses reflection to count non-nil fields; Validate() wraps the result in models.NewNillableGenericValidationError. Public Validate/ValidateWithComplexity always wrap exactly once.
patterns.example: if err := f.Validate(); err != nil { /* err is models.GenericValidationError */ }

patterns.name: Recursive And/Or with depth limit
patterns.description: Each filter type carries *[]FilterT And and *[]FilterT Or fields. validateWithComplexity(maxDepth) recurses and returns ErrFilterComplexityExceeded when maxDepth <= 0.

patterns.name: IsEmpty guards nil return from Select
patterns.description: Select returns nil for empty filters. Callers and implementations must check IsEmpty or nil before applying the predicate to an Ent selector.
patterns.example: if p := f.Select("field"); p != nil { query.Where(p) }

patterns.name: Contains uses ILIKE with escaped LIKE metacharacters
patterns.description: $contains and $ncontains wrap the value with EscapeLikePattern + ContainsPattern before passing to ILIKE/NOT ILIKE. Raw $like/$ilike operators pass values unescaped.

key_file_guides.role: All filter struct types plus the Filter interface. EscapeLikePattern, ContainsPattern, and ReverseContainsPattern are the LIKE-safety helpers.
key_file_guides.watch_for: FilterString has both $like (caller-controlled pattern) and $contains (auto-escaped plain substring). Do not double-escape by passing an already-percent-wrapped value to $contains.

key_file_guides.role: Dual-path tests: SelectWhereExpr (go-sqlbuilder) and Select (Ent) branches are validated side by side for every operator variant.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Test SQL strings differ between backends (? vs $1 placeholders, quoted identifiers). Use newSelectBuilder() for Ent assertions.

anti_patterns: Setting multiple operator fields on the same filter node (Validate returns ErrFilterMultipleOperators)
Applying Select() predicate without nil check — empty filter returns nil and will panic on the selector
Passing pre-escaped LIKE patterns to $contains/$ncontains — they will be double-escaped
Adding a new filter type without implementing both Select and SelectWhereExpr
Wrapping Validate() result in a second models.GenericValidationError — validateWithComplexity returns raw sentinels intentionally

decisions.decision: Dual output (Ent selector + go-sqlbuilder expression) on a single Filter type.
decisions.rationale: Domain adapters use Ent for Postgres and go-sqlbuilder for ClickHouse; one filter struct drives both backends without separate translation layers.

decisions.decision: Validation wraps errors in models.GenericValidationError exactly once at the public boundary.
decisions.rationale: Domain services call Validate() and forward the result directly to HTTP encoders that map GenericValidationError to 400; double-wrapping would break errors.Is checks.

code_examples.scenario: Apply a validated FilterString to an Ent query and a ClickHouse sqlbuilder query

pkg/gosundheit.purpose: Thin adapter that bridges the go-sundheit health check library to OpenMeter's structured slog logger. It implements health.CheckListener so health check lifecycle events (register, start, complete) emit structured log lines at appropriate levels.

patterns.name: Implement health.CheckListener via unexported struct
patterns.description: checkListener is unexported; consumers receive a health.CheckListener interface from NewLogger. Do not expose the concrete type.

patterns.name: Warn on error, Debug on success
patterns.description: OnCheckRegistered and OnCheckCompleted log at slog.Warn when result.Error != nil and at slog.Debug otherwise. Maintain this severity split for any new listener methods.
patterns.example: // Error path -> logger.Warn("health check failed", slog.String("check", name), slog.Any("error", result.Error))

key_file_guides.role: Sole file; declares checkListener and NewLogger. Only health.CheckListener interface is implemented.
key_file_guides.watch_for: go-sundheit's CheckListener interface may add methods in future versions — breaking the compile if not updated here.

anti_patterns: Logging at Error level for health check failures — the library handles alerting; Warn is intentional to avoid log-level escalation
Storing state in checkListener — it is a pure pass-through to the logger

pkg/hasher.purpose: Minimal hashing utility package: defines a Hash type alias (uint64), a Hasher interface, and a single xxhash-backed implementation. Used wherever a fast, non-cryptographic content hash is needed (e.g. lock key derivation in pkg/framework/lockr).

patterns.name: Hash = uint64 type alias
patterns.description: Hash is a type alias, not a named type — it is interchangeable with uint64 at the call site. Consumers that need nominal typing should define their own wrapper.

patterns.name: Hasher interface for injectable hashing
patterns.description: Structs that need to hash themselves implement Hasher by returning a Hash from Hash(). Inject via the interface, not via direct xxhash calls.
patterns.example:
type myKey struct{ ... }
func (k myKey) Hash() hasher.Hash { return hasher.NewHash([]byte(k.id)) }

key_file_guides.role: Defines Hash type and Hasher interface. Source of truth for the contract.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Hash is a type alias — changing it to a named type would be a breaking change for all callers assigning uint64 literals.

key_file_guides.role: NewHash([]byte) is the only concrete hash function. Uses cespare/xxhash v2.
key_file_guides.watch_for: xxhash is non-cryptographic — never use for security-sensitive hashing.

anti_patterns: Using hasher.NewHash for cryptographic purposes (passwords, signatures) — use crypto/sha256 or similar instead
Importing xxhash directly in domain packages instead of going through hasher.NewHash

pkg/idempotency.purpose: Single-purpose package that generates UUIDv7 idempotency keys for external API calls (e.g., Stripe). UUIDv7 provides monotonic, time-ordered keys that are safe to use as idempotency tokens without a shared counter.

patterns.name: Use Key() in production, MustKey() in tests/init
patterns.description: Key() returns (string, error) and should be used in application code paths. MustKey() panics on failure and is only appropriate in test setup or package-level init.

key_file_guides.role: Exports Key() and MustKey(). The entire package is this one file.
key_file_guides.watch_for: uuid.NewV7() can fail in environments with broken entropy sources — always handle the error in production paths.

anti_patterns: Using MustKey() in request-handling code paths — prefer Key() and propagate errors
Reusing the same key across multiple requests — each call to Key() must produce a fresh key

decisions.decision: UUIDv7 instead of UUIDv4 for idempotency keys.
decisions.rationale: UUIDv7 is time-ordered, making keys sortable and slightly more cache-friendly in database indexes while still being globally unique.
